channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Muturi Raises Concerns Over Governance of National Infrastructure Fund
item.description: Former Attorney General Justin Muturi has expressed serious concerns over the recently established National Infrastructure Fund, warning that its current structure could expose public funds to misuse ...

item.title: Warm Days and Frosty Nights: Why the UK Is Experiencing Big Temperature Swings This Spring
item.description: High pressure over the United Kingdom continues to bring mild, sunny conditions with above-average daytime temperatures. However, these clear skies also cause temperatures to plummet at night, produci...

item.title: Eid Celebrations Dampened by Conflict and Economic Hardship in Lebanon and the Middle East
item.description: This year, Eid al-Fitr is being observed under grim circumstances across Lebanon and other parts of the Middle East. In Lebanon, the ongoing conflict between Israel and Hezbollah has intensified, with...

item.title: Dave Grohl Reflects on Loss, Recovery, and Foo Fighters’ Return to Punk Roots
item.description: In a candid interview, Dave Grohl opens up about the personal and professional challenges that have shaped his recent work with Foo Fighters. Following the deaths of drummer Taylor Hawkins and Grohl’s...

item.title: Deputy Minister Mhlauli to Host Youth Services Expo in Kuruman
item.description: Deputy Minister in the Presidency, Nonceba Mhlauli, is set to host a Youth Services Expo at the Kuruman Town Hall in the Northern Cape on Friday, 20 March 2026. The initiative aims to bring essential ...

item.title: Tanzania Launches Comprehensive Tax Reforms to Improve Compliance and Investment Climate
item.description: President Samia Suluhu Hassan imeanza mchakato mkubwa wa kuboresha mfumo wa kodi nchini Tanzania, lengo kuu ikiwa ni kuimarisha imani ya wawekezaji, kurahisisha utekelezaji wa kodi, na kuhakikisha usa...

item.title: Eugene Wamalwa's Family Calls for Respect from President Ruto
item.description: The family of Eugene Wamalwa, leader of the Democratic Action Party of Kenya (DAP-K), has publicly appealed to President William Ruto to stop insulting and verbally attacking their son. In an intervie...

item.title: Coco Martin expresses heartfelt birthday greeting to Julia Montes
item.description: Filipino actor-director Coco Martin warmed the hearts of fans as he shared a touching birthday greeting for longtime partner Julia Montes. The post, shared through his official social media account, e...

item.title: ABS-CBN Music officially debuts The Red Strings, its first original Filipino band
item.description: ABS-CBN Music has officially introduced its first original Filipino music band, The Red Strings, marking a significant step in promoting OPM (Original Pilipino Music). Known as a leading media and ent...

item.title: Japanese PM Takaichi Meets Trump on Iran Strait Security Amid Historical Tensions
item.description: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi had a meeting with former US President Donald Trump in Washington on Thursday, successfully avoiding a confrontation over Iran. Trump praised Japan's support in ...

item.title: Canada urged to support military action to secure Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Adam Zivo argues that Canada has a strategic interest in supporting military efforts to end Iran's blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. The article highlights that Iran’s pursuit of nuclear weapons has d...

item.title: Progress on Cape Town’s Inner-City Affordable Housing Projects Remains Slow
item.description: The Western Cape government’s major affordable housing initiatives in Cape Town’s inner city are slowly moving forward, though construction has not yet started. Premier Alan Winde, Mayor Geordin Hill-...

item.title: Tourism Advocate Urges Stronger LGU and Police Coordination to End Illegal Gambling in Pangasinan
item.description: A local tourism advocate in Pangasinan has called for stronger collaboration between police authorities and local government units (LGUs) to put an end to the widespread problem of illegal gambling in...

item.title: How Generation Z is reshaping Kenya's political awareness and activism
item.description: The article interrogates whether Kenya’s Generation Z—mostly young people born roughly between the late 1990s and early 2000s—will ultimately transform how an entire generation thinks about politics. ...

item.title: House Bill Proposes One-Year Moratorium on New Taxes Due to Rising Fuel Costs
item.description: House Deputy Speaker David “Jayjay” Suarez of Quezon has filed House Bill No. 8660, also known as the Tax Relief Act of 2026, seeking to suspend the imposition of new national and local taxes for the ...

item.title: Pakistan Air Traffic Controllers Divert 30 Aircraft Away from Iranian Conflict Zone
item.description: LAHORE: The Pakistan Airport Authority (PAA) successfully prevented around 30 passenger planes from accidentally entering the Iranian war zone, thanks to the vigilant efforts of Lahore and Karachi air...

item.title: Nigeria and UK agree to streamline deportation process for Nigerians in the UK without legal status
item.description: Na so Nigeria don enter one new agreement with UK wey go make am easier to deport Nigerians wey dey the UK illegally. This one cover three main group of people: those wey don overstay their visa, thos...

item.title: Kent outbreak: Health officials investigate 27 cases of meningitis B, issue warnings and vaccination drive
item.description: Health authorities are investigating 27 confirmed cases of meningitis linked to an outbreak in Kent, England, up from 20 cases previously. The outbreak, which has resulted in two deaths, has affected ...

item.title: Stormers focus on staying grounded ahead of Dragons clash after Bulls win
item.description: The Stormers are determined to keep their feet on the ground following a dominant derby victory over the Bulls at Loftus Versfeld, where they secured a 32-19 bonus-point win. Team captain Ruhan Nel ha...

item.title: President Tinubu and First Lady Return From UK, Plan to Observe Sallah Holiday in Lagos
item.description: President Bola Ahmed Tinubu and First Lady Remi Tinubu don return to Nigeria after him official visit to the United Kingdom. The visit, which na historic one, last for two days and e mark the first ti...
